headlines.text: Houthis fire first ballistic missile at Israel, officially entering the Iran war on Day 29
'No Kings' protests erupt across all 50 states — organizers claim it could be the biggest in U.S. history
Israel escalates strikes on Iranian nuclear sites and industrial plants
Trump extends Iran's Strait of Hormuz deadline 10 more days to April 6, calls talks 'very well'
Dow and Nasdaq enter correction territory as Wall Street logs fifth straight losing week
National gas average hits $3.98 — up a full dollar in a single month
Rubio at G7 in France struggles to sell allies on U.S. Iran war strategy
Tiger Woods arrested for DUI in Jupiter, Florida — mug shot released
'Project Hail Mary' starring Ryan Gosling holds strong at box office in second weekend
Saudi Arabia intercepts ballistic missile aimed at Riyadh; Kuwait airport radar damaged by drone strike

breaking.text: Houthis confirm first missile strike on Israel — ballistic missile intercepted over occupied West Bank

stories.hed: Houthis Finally Show Up to the Party, Houthi-Style
stories.dek: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels launched their first ballistic missile at Israeli military sites, officially widening the war to a fourth front. Meanwhile Israel threatened to 'escalate and expand' strikes on Tehran, and Trump — ever the dealmaker — extended his own deadline for the third time.

stories.hed: Rubio Flies to France, G7 Allies Decline to Co-Sign U.S. Iran War
stories.dek: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with G7 foreign ministers at a 12th-century abbey outside Paris, where almost every ally has raised objections to U.S. strategy in Iran. The group photo was taken in silence — which, diplomatically speaking, says everything.

stories.hed: Millions March in 'No Kings' Protests Nationwide — and Abroad
stories.dek: More than 3,100 'No Kings' demonstrations are underway across the U.S. today, with Bruce Springsteen performing at the flagship rally in Minnesota and two-thirds of RSVPs coming from outside major cities. Even Madrid and Paris got in on it, because apparently nobody likes this.

stories.hed: Democrats Accuse Trump of Engineering 2026 Midterms With 'SAVE America Act' Voter ID Push
stories.dek: Trump is pushing the renamed SAVE America Act — a voter ID bill requiring proof of citizenship to register — and told House Republicans it will 'guarantee the midterms.' Democrats see a master plan; Republicans see a messaging win; voters see a very long line at the DMV.

stories.hed: Oil Just Hit $100 a Barrel. Wall Street Is Having Its Worst Month Since 2022. And Trump Says Talks Are Going 'Very Well.'
stories.dek: WTI crude briefly touched $100 a barrel for the first time since 2022, the S&P 500 just closed out its fifth consecutive losing week, and Iran says there are zero negotiations happening. Other than that, things are going great.

markets.context: Stocks logged their fifth straight losing week as WTI briefly topped $100 a barrel and Iran rejected direct peace talks, sending investors fleeing into gold and out of everything else.

gas.note: Up a full dollar in one month — your car is now officially a luxury good.

popCulture.hed: Tiger Woods Arrested for DUI in Florida After Crashing His Car
popCulture.dek: Tiger Woods was arrested Friday in Jupiter, Florida after police say he clipped a car and flipped his vehicle — his mug shot was released Saturday morning. It's not the comeback anyone was expecting from a man who survived a near-fatal crash in 2021.

genZ.definition: A deeply cursed Italian brainrot meme featuring an Indonesian wooden plank character that 'strikes' at the most inconvenient moments — mid-workout, mid-assignment, mid-anything. The horror is in the randomness: you're just living your life and then, without warning, Tung Tung Tung Sahur.
genZ.usage: Me trying to follow Trump's Iran deadline extension timeline: *Tung Tung Tung Sahur enters the chat for the third time this month.*

tldr: Day 29 of the Iran war got messier as Houthis fired their first missile at Israel, WTI oil briefly hit $100 a barrel, and millions of Americans took to the streets in the largest 'No Kings' protest yet — all while Trump extended his own deadline for the third time and called it progress.
